title: StringHeap assertion failure (`cell_base_ == old`) when compiling large Verilog files
body:
I have recently been experimenting with using Yosys to optimize Verilog code, and then simulating the optimized code with Icarus Verilog (iverilog). However, when I run simulations on the optimized code, I occasionally encounter an assertion failure from iverilog:

```
ivl: ./libmisc/StringHeap.cc:77: const char* StringHeap::add(const char*): Assertion `cell_base_ == old' failed.
Aborted (core dumped)
exit status 134
```

This assertion error tends to occur when the design is relatively complex — for example, when the original Verilog code consists of a few hundred lines of complex logic, and after optimization, it becomes several thousand lines of simpler code.  
I tried using `sv-bugpoint` and `creduce` to minimize the code that triggers the failure, but both tools were unsuccessful.  
Therefore, it seems the problem only arises when the code is sufficiently large or complex.

Here is the file that triggers the bug:  
[[bug.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19928628/bug.txt)]

The steps to reproduce are:

```
iverilog -g2012 bug.v
```

Since the file contains several thousand lines of code, it may be difficult for developers to pinpoint and fix the issue immediately.  
However, I believe it would still be valuable to share this information and make others aware of the problem. :)
code_blocks:
ivl: ./libmisc/StringHeap.cc:77: const char* StringHeap::add(const char*): Assertion `cell_base_ == old' failed.
Aborted (core dumped)
exit status 134
